exercises.instruction: Reading Comprehension: Read the text about a teenage girl's hobby and answer the questions with True, False, or Not Given.
exercises.body: My name is Sarah and I am 14 years old. My favorite hobby is photography. I started taking photos three years ago when my parents gave me a camera for my birthday. I love taking pictures of nature, especially forests and mountains. Last summer, I went to the Tatra Mountains and took over 200 photos of landscapes. I don't have a professional camera, but I use a special app to make my photos look better. My best friend, Emma, also likes photography, but she prefers taking portraits of people. We often go to the park together on Saturdays to practice. I want to become a professional photographer in the future because I believe it is the most exciting job in the world.

exercises.instruction:
Reading Questions:
1. Sarah has been taking photos for three years.
2. Sarah prefers taking photos of people.
3. Sarah used a professional camera in the Tatra Mountains.
4. Sarah and Emma go to the park every Sunday.
5. Sarah wants to work as a photographer in the future.
Grammar: Present Simple. Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verb in brackets.
exercises.body:
1. My brother _______ (play) football every Tuesday.
2. We _______ (not / like) eating broccoli.
3. _______ she _______ (speak) English fluently?
4. The train to London _______ (leave) at 8:00 AM.
5. I usually _______ (get up) at 7 o'clock.

exercises.instruction: Grammar: Past Simple. Change the sentences from the present to the past.
exercises.body:
1. I go to the cinema. -> I _______ to the cinema.
2. She eats an apple. -> She _______ an apple.
3. We are happy. -> We _______ happy.
4. He doesn't play tennis. -> He _______ tennis.
5. Do you see the bird? -> _______ you _______ the bird?

exercises.instruction: Grammar: Future Simple. Complete the sentences using 'will' or 'won't' and the verb in brackets.
exercises.body:
1. I think it _______ (rain) tomorrow.
2. I promise I _______ (help) you with your homework.
3. Maybe we _______ (go) to Italy next summer.
4. I'm sure he _______ (not / forget) your birthday.
5. _______ you _______ (close) the window, please?

exercises.instruction: Vocabulary: Matching. Match the words with their opposites.
exercises.body:
1. Big      a) Slow
2. Fast     b) Small
3. Happy    c) Cold
4. Hot      d) Sad
5. Easy     e) Difficult

Exercise 1 & 2: 1. True, 2. False, 3. False, 4. False, 5. True
Exercise 3: 1. plays, 2. don't like, 3. Does... speak, 4. leaves, 5. get up
Exercise 4: 1. went, 2. ate, 3. were, 4. didn't play, 5. Did... see
Exercise 5: 1. will rain, 2. will help, 3. will go, 4. won't forget, 5. Will... close
Exercise 6: 1-b, 2-a, 3-d, 4-c, 5-e
